mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in the unemployment rate below 4.0% signals tightening labor market conditions that boost corporate profit margins, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns are negative in 70%+ of instances where UNRATE falls below 4.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in residential construction (HOUST) and real retail sales (RSAFS) signals expanding consumer demand that drives equity earnings upgrades, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day SPX returns are negative when both HOUST and RSAFS show positive month-over-month growth.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in industrial production (INDPRO) below its 12-month moving average signals weakening real economic activity that triggers risk-off sentiment, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns are positive in 60%+ of instances where INDPRO falls below its 12-month moving average.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the CPI (CPIAUCSL) above 2.5% signals inflationary pressures that prompt central bank rate hikes, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns are positive in 70%+ of instances where CPIAUCSL exceeds 2.5%.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) below 50k signals weak labor market performance that triggers equity sell-offs, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns are positive in 60%+ of instances where PAYEMS falls below 50k.

mechanism_narrative: A concurrent decline in GDP (GDPC1) and industrial production (INDPRO) signals recessionary risks that drive equity market declines, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns are positive when both GDPC1 and INDPRO show negative growth.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in industrial production (INDPRO) above its 3-month moving average signals strengthening supply-side dynamics that drive equity earnings growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns are negative in 70%+ of instances where INDPRO exceeds its 3-month moving average.
